title: Content Codings, Character Sets, Media Admission, and Digests · Go Retrieval

Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for representation decoding and identity ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decoded, retained, parsed, and extracted sizes and stop before resource ceilings.

Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Wire Decoded Budgets · changed case · Review Wire Decoded Budgets
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 1176 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Stage Digests, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Stage Digests record says representation decoding and identity ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Stage Digests.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Stage Digests for representation decoding and identity ledger.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Stage Digests
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Stage Digests: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 3509 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep raw, decoded, tree, main-content, record, and rendered digests separate with algorithm and version.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Stage Digests.
steps.why: Retrieving Stage Digests through the concrete representation decoding and identity ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Stage Digests, keep Stage Digests connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep raw, decoded, tree, main-content, record, and rendered digests separate with algorithm and version.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Stage Digests, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 3509 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep raw, decoded, tree, main-content, record, and rendered digests separate with algorithm and version.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Stage Digests, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One SHA-256 value is complete representation identity.
